content: orch-go tmux spawn is fire-and-forget - no session ID capture
reason: opencode run --attach is TUI-based; --format json gives session ID but loses TUI. Accept title-matching via orch status for monitoring.

content: debugging Insufficient Balance error when orch usage showed 99% remaining
result: was checking wrong thing - the OpenCode SERVER version matters, not CLI. Dev server (0.0.0-dev from Dec 12) had stale auth. Fix: restart server with current version.

content: Opus default, Gemini escape hatch
reason: 2 Claude Max subscriptions (covered), Gemini is pay-per-token + tier 2 TPM. Escalation: 1) Opus default, 2) account switch, 3) --model flash

content: Add Leave it Better after self-review, before completion criteria
reason: Natural position in workflow - self-review validates quality, Leave it Better captures knowledge, completion gates exit

content: Auto-generated skills require template edits
reason: Direct edits to SKILL.md will be overwritten by build process - must edit src/SKILL.md.template and src/phases/*.md

content: kb search and rg are complementary tools
reason: kb searches knowledge artifacts (.kb/), rg searches code - agents should use both strategically based on query type

content: Headless Swarm = batch execution + rate-limit management across accounts
reason: User clarified scope: focus on concurrent agent spawning with capacity management, not distributed architecture or multi-model routing

content: Concurrent agents trigger TPM throttling at >60% session usage
reason: Observed performance degradation and user reports of hitting limits during swarm operations

content: Registry respawn workflow uses slot reuse pattern
reason: Preserves single-entry-per-ID invariant while enabling abandon→respawn lifecycle

content: Registry updates must happen before beads close in orch complete
reason: Prevents inconsistent state where beads shows closed but registry shows active

content: Session idle ≠ agent complete
reason: Agents legitimately go idle during normal operation (loading, thinking, tool execution)

content: orch spawn context delivery is reliable
reason: Verified that SPAWN_CONTEXT.md is correctly populated and accessible by the agent

content: Use IsOpenCodeReady for tmux TUI detection
reason: Reliably detects when OpenCode TUI is ready for input in a tmux pane, avoiding race conditions.

content: Create new beads issue if provided ID is invalid
reason: Protocol requires phase reporting; invalid ID blocks this. Creating a new issue allows compliance.

content: orch tail on tmux agent
result: Currently fails because it expects a SessionID (headless mode) and doesn't fall back to tmux capture-pane

content: Refactor shell-out functions for testability
reason: Extracting command construction into Build*Command functions allows unit testing of logic without side effects

content: Use Text field for verify.Comment
reason: verify.Comment struct uses Text field, not Content

content: verify.Comment uses Text field
reason: Matches bd CLI JSON output

content: orch-go CLI independence
reason: CLI commands connect directly to OpenCode (4096), not orch serve (3333)

content: Use build/orch for serve daemon
reason: Prevents SIGKILL during make install

content: VerifyCompletion relies on latest beads comment
reason: Ensures current state is validated

content: tmux fallback requires either current registry window ID OR beads ID in window name format [beads-id]
reason: If both are stale/missing, fallback fails despite window existing

content: tmux fallback requires either current registry window ID OR beads ID in window name format [beads-id]
reason: Both paths needed for resilience; if both stale/missing, fallback fails despite window existing

content: Iteration 8 tmux fallback verification successful
reason: After attach mode implementation (7ca8438), all three fallback mechanisms (tail, question, status) continue to work correctly with no regressions

content: Tmux fallback requires at least one valid path: current registry window_id OR beads ID in window name format [beads-id]
reason: Dual dependency failure causes fallback to fail even when window exists (discovered iteration 5, confirmed iteration 10)

content: orch tail tmux fallback requires either current registry window ID OR beads ID in window name format [beads-id]
reason: Dual-dependency failure causes fallback to fail when both are stale/missing

content: orch-go DefaultModel should be Opus (claude-opus-4-5-20251101), not Gemini
reason: Orchestrator guidance expects Opus for complex work, current Gemini default conflicts with operational practice

content: Using empty string model resolution to let opencode decide
result: orch-go Resolve('') returns hardcoded DefaultModel instead of omitting flag

content: orch-go agent state exists in four layers (OpenCode memory, OpenCode disk, registry, tmux)
reason: Each layer has independent lifecycle - cleanup must touch all layers or ghosts accumulate

content: OpenCode ListSessions WITH x-opencode-directory header returns disk sessions, WITHOUT returns in-memory
reason: Finding from investigation - explains 2 vs 238 session count discrepancy

content: orch clean to remove ghost sessions automatically
result: Only touches registry, doesn't verify tmux windows exist or OpenCode sessions are live

content: Agents must not spawn more than 3 iterations without human review
reason: Prevents runaway iteration loops like 12 tmux fallback tests in 9 minutes

content: orch complete must verify SYNTHESIS.md exists and is not placeholder before closing issue
reason: 70% of agents completed without synthesis in 24h chaos period

content: Implement 3-tier guardrail system: preflight checks, completion gates, daily reconciliation
reason: Post-mortem showed 115 commits in 24h with 7 missing guardrails enabling runaway automation

content: Port allocation should use ranges by purpose (vite: 5173-5199, api: 3333-3399)
reason: Prevents conflicts and makes purpose clear from port number

content: orch init must be idempotent - safe to run multiple times
reason: Prevents accidental overwrites and enables 'run init to update' pattern

content: Investigations live in .kb/ not workspaces
reason: kb context discoverability essential; SYNTHESIS.md bridges via investigation_path pointer

content: Orchestrator sessions need SESSION_HANDOFF.md
reason: Session amnesia applies to orchestrator work; skillc pattern provides mature template

content: Session boundaries have three distinct patterns: worker (protocol-driven via Phase:Complete), orchestrator (state-driven via session-transition), and cross-session (manual via SESSION_HANDOFF.md)
reason: Investigation found no unified boundary protocol; each type optimized for its context

content: Multi-agent synthesis relies on workspace isolation + SYNTHESIS.md + orch review
reason: 100 commits, 52 synthesis files, 0 conflicts validates current architecture

content: Failure artifact capture should be layered: automatic events, optional reason flag, manual post-mortems
reason: Balance between context capture and workflow efficiency - most abandons don't need detailed post-mortems

content: orch-go is primary CLI, orch-cli (Python) is reference/fallback
reason: Go provides better primitives (single binary, OpenCode HTTP client, goroutines); Python taught requirements through 27k lines and 200+ investigations

content: Registry is caching layer, not source of truth - all data exists in OpenCode/tmux/beads
reason: Investigation found all registry data can be derived from primary sources

content: Three-tier temporal model (ephemeral/persistent/operational) organizes artifact placement
reason: Artifacts live where their lifecycle dictates - session-bound to workspace, project-lifetime to kb, work-in-progress to beads

content: D.E.K.N. is universal handoff structure
reason: Delta/Evidence/Knowledge/Next enables 30-second context transfer between Claude instances - proven across SYNTHESIS.md, investigations, and session handoffs

content: Session_id stored in workspace file not registry
reason: Co-locates data with workspace, single writer, no lock contention

content: Use content parsing not frontmatter for kb-internal citations
reason: Zero maintenance, already works via grep, adequate performance at 138 files

content: Temporal density and repeated constraints are highest value reflection signals
reason: Low noise, high actionability - tested against real kn/kb data

content: Constraint validity tested by implementation, not age
reason: Constraints become outdated when code contradicts them (implementation supersession), not when time passes. Test with rg before assuming constraint holds.

content: Chronicle should be a view over existing artifacts, not new artifact type
reason: Minimal taxonomy principle; source data already exists in git/kn/kb; value is in narrative synthesis not data capture

content: Reflection value comes from orchestrator review + follow-up, not execution-time process changes
reason: Evidence: post-synthesis reflection with Dylan created orch-go-ws4z epic (6 children) from captured questions

content: Session ID resolution pattern
reason: Commands that need to find agents should use resolveSessionID or the runTail pattern: workspace files first, then API lookup, then tmux fallback

content: kb reflect uses single command with --type flag for four reflection modes
reason: Consistent with kb pattern, most discoverable, extensible. Maps directly to signals from ws4z investigations.

content: Self-reflection is signal-triggered not time-scheduled
reason: Density thresholds (3+ investigations) produce actionable signals; time intervals (weekly review) produce noise. Per ws4z.8 investigation.

content: Dual-mode architecture (tmux for visual, HTTP for programmatic) is the correct design
reason: Investigation confirmed each mode serves distinct, irreplaceable needs

content: Cross-project epics use Option A: epic in primary repo, ad-hoc spawns with --no-track in secondary repos, manual bd close with commit refs
reason: Only working pattern today. Beads multi-repo hydration is read-only aggregation, bd repo commands are buggy.

content: Beads multi-repo config via bd repo add
result: JSON parsing error even after setting repos config. bd repo commands are buggy in v0.29.0

content: Beads OSS: Clean Slate over Fork
reason: Local features (ai-help, health, tree) not used by orch ecosystem. Drop rather than maintain.

content: Beads local-features branch
result: Features built speculatively but never integrated. Caused rebase conflicts with zero benefit.

content: OpenCode x-opencode-directory header returns ALL disk sessions, not just matching ones
reason: API behavior is counterintuitive - without header returns in-memory only

content: Post-registry lifecycle uses 4 state sources: OpenCode sessions, tmux windows, beads issues, workspaces
reason: Registry removed due to false positive completion detection; derived lookups replace central state

content: orch status can show phantom agents (tmux windows where OpenCode exited)
reason: No reconciliation between tmux liveness and OpenCode session state

content: Deleting orphaned beads dependencies with bd-* prefix
result: Cannot restore - issues belong to different repo

content: Beads cross-repo contamination can create orphaned FK references
reason: bd-* prefixed dependencies were found in orch-go database from separate beads repo

content: Confidence scores in investigations are noise, not signal
reason: Nov 2025 Codex case: 5 investigations at High/Very High confidence all reached wrong conclusions. Current distribution: 90% cluster at 85-95%, providing no discriminative value. Recommendation: Replace with structured uncertainty (tested/untested enumeration).

content: Use phased migration for skillc skill management
reason: Incremental approach allows validation at each step and maintains backward compatibility with existing skills

content: skillc and orch build skills are complementary, not competing
reason: skillc compiles project-local .skillc/ to CLAUDE.md; orch build skills compiles templated skills to ~/.claude/skills/. Different purposes, both needed.

content: Template ownership: kb-cli owns artifact templates (investigation/decision/guide), orch-go owns spawn-time templates (SYNTHESIS/SPAWN_CONTEXT/FAILURE_REPORT)
reason: Prevents drift by establishing clear domains. See .kb/investigations/2025-12-22-inv-deep-dive-template-system-fragmentation.md

content: Tmux spawn uses opencode attach mode
reason: Enables dual TUI+API access - sessions visible via orch status while still showing TUI for visual monitoring

content: Use inline lineage metadata for project extraction, not centralized registry
reason: Session Amnesia principle requires self-describing artifacts; centralized registry creates fragile external dependency

content: beads progress tracking for untracked agents
result: bd comment fails when --no-track generates placeholder ID instead of real issue

content: orch status counts ALL workers-* tmux windows as active
reason: Discovered during phantom agent investigation - status inflated by persistent windows

content: Pre-spawn kb context should filter to orch ecosystem repos
reason: 33% of global results are noise from unrelated repos (price-watch, dotfiles). Filtering preserves cross-repo signal while eliminating noise.

content: skillc cannot compile SKILL.md templates without template expansion feature
reason: orch-knowledge skills use SKILL-TEMPLATE markers that require regex substitution not concatenation

content: orch complete auto-closes tmux window after successful verification
reason: Complete means done - window goes away, beads closes, workspace remains. Prevents phantom accumulation (41 windows today). Debugging escape hatch: don't complete until ready to close.

content: orch-knowledge repo is at ~/orch-knowledge (not ~/Documents/personal/orch-knowledge)
reason: Agents kept failing to find it when given relative path. Skill sources live at ~/orch-knowledge/skills/src/worker/{skill}/.skillc/

content: D.E.K.N. 'Next:' field must be updated when marking Status: Complete
reason: Prevents stale investigations that mislead future agents

content: orch status shows PHASE and TASK columns from beads data
reason: Makes output actionable - users can immediately see what each agent is doing

content: kb-cli owns artifact templates (investigation, decision, guide, research)
reason: Consolidation complete - skill templates/ directories removed, kb-cli hardcoded updated with D.E.K.N.

content: Template ownership split by domain
reason: kb-cli owns knowledge artifacts (investigation, decision, guide, research); orch-go owns orchestration artifacts (SYNTHESIS, SPAWN_CONTEXT, FAILURE_REPORT, SESSION_HANDOFF)

content: Always use findWorkspaceByBeadsID() for beads ID to workspace lookups
reason: Workspace names don't contain beads ID - the ID only exists in SPAWN_CONTEXT.md

content: SPAWN_CONTEXT.md is 100% redundant - generated from beads + kb context + skill + template
reason: Investigation confirmed all content exists elsewhere and can be regenerated at spawn time

content: orch init delegates .kb/ creation to kb init command
reason: Ensures consistency with kb's own initialization logic and avoids duplicating directory structure knowledge

content: ECOSYSTEM.md location is ~/.orch/ not ~/.claude/
reason: Keeps orchestration docs with orchestration state; ~/.claude/ is for Claude-specific config

content: Progressive disclosure for skill bloat
reason: 89% of feature-impl spawns use only 2-3 phases. Extract detailed phase guidance to reference docs, keep core workflow inline. Proven pattern from 2025-11-21 instruction optimization. Reduces 1757→~500 lines.

content: Skillc embeds ALL template_sources unconditionally
reason: No mechanism exists for conditional inclusion at compile time. Solution must work at spawn-time (runtime reference) not compile-time (conditional includes).

content: Progressive disclosure for skill bloat
reason: 77% reduction (1757→400 lines) while preserving all guidance in reference docs

content: External integrations require manual smoke test before Phase: Complete
reason: OAuth feature shipped with passing tests but failed real-world use. Tests couldn't cover actual OAuth flow with Anthropic.

content: OpenCode attach mode only creates sessions after first message received
reason: TUI startup is not session creation - must send prompt before looking up session ID

content: Stall detection: session.status=busy for >5min without message.part events indicates hung Claude API call
reason: SSE monitoring pattern - healthy sessions emit regular message.part.updated events

content: CompletionService bridges SSE completion detection and slot management for headless agents
reason: Headless agents need automatic slot release when they complete; Monitor detects completions, WorkerPool manages slots, CompletionService links them

content: orch-init-claudemd-already-implemented
reason: orch init already creates CLAUDE.md by default with auto-detection - implemented in og-feat-implement-orch-init-21dec

content: beads output format uses ' - ' separator for title
reason: All bd commands (list, ready) use this format: {beads-id} [{priority}] [{type}] {status} ... - {title}

content: Tiered spawn protocol uses .tier file in workspace for orch complete
reason: Allows VerifyCompletion to read tier from workspace and skip SYNTHESIS.md requirement for light-tier spawns without requiring orchestrator to pass tier explicitly

content: kb templates use inline lineage metadata (extracted-from, supersedes, superseded-by)
reason: Session Amnesia principle requires self-describing artifacts; headers appear in templates at ~/.kb/templates/

content: Domain-based template ownership: kb-cli owns artifact templates (investigation, decision, guide), orch-go owns spawn-time templates (SYNTHESIS, SPAWN_CONTEXT, FAILURE_REPORT)
reason: Validated during stale template retirement - skill templates directories are orphaned when kb create provides templates

content: kb-cli templates should use {{title}} and {{date}} mustache-style placeholders
reason: These are replaced by applyTemplate() function - ensures consistency between file templates and fallback consts

content: When spawned for cross-repo work, verify work completion status before starting
reason: Task orch-go-oo1f: spawned in orch-go for work in orch-knowledge. Template was already retired (commit 7430185) before agent fully engaged. Quick verification could have saved agent context.

content: kb-cli investigationTemplate is synced with ~/.kb/templates/INVESTIGATION.md
reason: Updated hardcoded fallback const to match canonical template (234 lines with D.E.K.N., Lineage, Implementation Recommendations). Commit 032ea18 in kb-cli.

content: Multi-repo hydration requires healthy database
reason: Orphaned dependencies in kb-cli blocked all database operations including multi-repo sync. Fix with 'bd doctor --fix' before attempting multi-repo setup.

content: kb-cli templates are source of truth, ~/.kb/templates are runtime copies
reason: kb binary embeds templates from create.go; runtime templates in ~/.kb/templates/ get overwritten on kb builds; changes must go in kb-cli source

content: Beads multi-repo hydration works correctly in v0.33.2
reason: Config disconnect bug fixed in commit 634c0b93. Prior kn entry about 'buggy v0.29.0' is superseded.

content: bd multi-repo config is YAML-only, database config is legacy
reason: Fix commit 634c0b93 moved repos config from database to YAML. GetMultiRepoConfig() reads YAML only. Stale binary causes silent failure.

content: Tmux is the default spawn mode in orch-go, not headless
reason: Testing and code inspection confirmed tmux is default (main.go:1042), CLAUDE.md documentation was incorrect

content: Untracked spawns (--no-track) generate placeholder beads IDs that fail bd comment commands
reason: Beads IDs like orch-go-untracked-* don't exist in database, causing bd comment to fail with 'issue not found' - this is expected behavior, not a bug

content: Headless spawn mode is production-ready
reason: All 5 requirements verified working: status detection, monitoring, completion detection, error handling, user visibility. Investigation orch-go-0r2q confirmed no blockers exist.

content: CreateSession API now accepts model parameter for headless spawns
reason: Headless mode was missing model selection capability - added Model field to CreateSessionRequest struct and threaded through CreateSession function to achieve parity with inline/tmux modes

content: Use existing stores for UI improvements over backend changes
reason: All necessary data (errorEvents, agentlogEvents) already exists in frontend stores; UI-only changes are faster to implement and deploy

content: Dashboard event panels max-h-64 for visibility without overwhelming layout
reason: Doubled from 32px provides better event scanning while preserving agent grid visibility

content: Default spawn mode is headless with --tmux opt-in
reason: Aligns implementation with documentation (CLAUDE.md, orchestrator skill), reduces TUI overhead for automation, tmux still available via explicit flag

content: Document all spawn modes in CLAUDE.md
reason: Users need to see all options (headless default, --tmux opt-in, --inline blocking) to make informed spawn choices

content: Help text should be organized by mode hierarchy (default → opt-in → blocking)
reason: Users need to see default behavior first; mode grouping in examples improves discoverability and understanding

content: Real-time UI updates via client-side SSE parsing
reason: Parsing SSE events client-side (rather than polling API) provides instant updates without backend changes and scales better with many agents

content: Activity state should be ephemeral in UI
reason: Real-time activity is meant to show current state, not history - keeping it ephemeral avoids state management complexity and storage costs

content: Use tmux-centric CLI commands for cross-project server management
reason: Leverages existing port registry and tmuxinator infrastructure, fits developer workflow, delivers immediate value with minimal code (~200 lines)

content: Researching Foreman, Overmind, and Nx for polyrepo server management
result: These tools target single-project Procfiles or monorepo orchestration, not cross-project polyrepo coordination

content: Spawn system verified functional for basic use cases
reason: Test spawn successfully created workspace, loaded context, created investigation file via kb CLI

content: orch servers uses project-grouped output not service-grouped
reason: Showing ports grouped by project (e.g., 'web:5173, api:3333') is more intuitive than separate rows per service because developers think in terms of projects not individual services

content: orch servers open targets web port only
reason: Opening both web and api ports in browser is noisy; web port (vite/dev server) is the primary entry point for local development

content: Epics with parallel component work must include a final integration child issue
reason: Swarm agents build components in parallel but nothing wires them together. Without explicit integration issue, manual intervention needed to create runnable feature. Learned from pw-4znt where 8 components built but no route existed.

content: Agents need visibility into remaining context tokens
reason: Currently flying blind - no warning when approaching limits, no graceful degradation. Should know when to wrap up, prioritize deliverables over nice-to-haves, or spawn child for remaining work.

content: Use OpenCode's built-in glob/grep tools instead of installing fd
reason: glob/grep already use ripgrep internally (5-37x faster than find). Installing fd would be redundant and doesn't solve root cause (agents using wrong tool)

content: Document existing capabilities before building new infrastructure
reason: WebFetch investigation showed tool already exists - main gap was documentation not capability

content: Use phased adversarial verification over post-completion review
reason: Post-completion review doesn't prevent validation loops (Budden case), only detects after agent committed to flawed conclusions

content: Verification cannot rely solely on AI-generated explanations
reason: AI explanations increase trust even when wrong; experts can rationalize AI outputs creating validation loops (Nate Jones article)

content: Do AI validation loops occur in typical development work or only in high-complexity domains like mathematical proofs?
Tmux session existence is correct abstraction for 'orch servers' status - separate infrastructure from project servers
reason: orch serve is persistent monitoring infrastructure, not ephemeral project server. Tmux lifecycle matches dev server lifecycle. Mixing them creates semantic confusion and false status reporting.

content: Dashboard should use progressive disclosure (Active/Recent/Archive sections) for session management
reason: Balances operational visibility (active work always visible) with historical debugging (expand sections as needed) and UI clarity (collapsed sections reduce clutter). Only approach that satisfies all three user contexts: development focus, debugging history, and health monitoring.

content: OpenCode model selection is per-message, not per-session
reason: Intentional design enabling mid-conversation model switching, cost optimization, and flexibility. Pass model to prompt/message calls, not session creation.

content: Worker spawns must set ORCH_WORKER=1 to skip orchestrator skill loading
reason: Orchestrator skill (1,251 lines ~37k tokens) is auto-loaded by session-context plugin for all orch projects but is unnecessary for worker sessions, wastes context budget

content: Worker agents must test spawn functionality via unit tests and code review, not end-to-end spawning
reason: Prevents recursive spawn testing incidents while still enabling verification

content: kb context command hangs on some queries
reason: Blocks orch spawn from returning, use --skip-artifact-check as workaround

content: Binary staleness should be prevented with make install
reason: Project tracks ./orch binary in git, but build process creates build/orch. Users should run 'make install' to sync ~/bin/orch rather than manually copying to root directory.

content: Use x-opencode-env-ORCH_WORKER header for headless spawns
reason: OpenCode HTTP API doesn't support env vars in request body, but follows x-opencode-* header pattern like x-opencode-directory, so we use x-opencode-env-ORCH_WORKER: 1 header when creating sessions via CreateSession API call

content: ORCH_WORKER=1 set via environment inheritance on OpenCode server start and cmd.Env for direct spawns
reason: Headless spawns use HTTP API where env can't be passed directly, but agents inherit env from server process. For inline/tmux, use cmd.Env on exec.Cmd.

content: Root main.go should be removed from orch-go
reason: It's a duplicate of legacy/main.go, deprecated per README, and building from root creates a non-functional CLI

content: Build artifacts should not be committed at project root
reason: Development executables (orch-*, test-*) clutter the directory and waste space (~75MB+)

content: Use CLI subprocess for headless spawns
reason: OpenCode HTTP API ignores model parameter; CLI (opencode run --model) is the only way to specify models

content: OpenCode auto-installs plugin dependencies
result: Skipped in local dev mode (opencode-dev); plugins fail to import when @opencode-ai/plugin not installed

content: Skill constraints use HTML comment blocks for embedding
reason: Simple extraction via regex, survives markdown processing, follows existing skillc pattern for metadata (checksum, source)

content: Layer 2 skill constraint verification already implemented in orch-go
reason: pkg/verify/constraint.go (211 lines) parses SKILL-CONSTRAINTS block from SPAWN_CONTEXT.md and verifies required/optional patterns via VerifyCompletionFull()

content: Skills performing well after Dec 20-23 changes
reason: SYNTHESIS rates high (~80%), progressive disclosure reduced feature-impl 77% without quality loss, light-tier spawn system working correctly

content: Skill constraints use spawn time mtime filtering
reason: Prevents false positives from previous spawns matching patterns like {date}-inv-*.md - now only files created after spawn timestamp are matched

content: orch serve displayThreshold should match orch status (30min)
reason: 6h threshold showed 25 stale sessions while orch status showed 0, causing dashboard noise

content: Phase gates use SKILL-PHASES HTML comment block in SPAWN_CONTEXT.md
reason: Consistent with constraint pattern, declarative, backwards compatible

content: Skill-driven context injection uses HTML comment parsing for SKILL-REQUIRES blocks
reason: Mirrors Layer 1 SKILL-CONSTRAINTS pattern, enables backwards compatibility, and allows easy extraction without YAML parsing

content: D.E.K.N. validation gates file output only
reason: Allows draft preview workflow - users see prompts with stdout, then fill in content before saving with -o

content: Use '-' as display fallback for missing duration data in UI
reason: Cleaner than 'N/A' or empty, signals missing data without breaking layout

content: kb context uses keyword matching, not semantic understanding - 'how would the system recommend...' questions require orchestrator synthesis
reason: Tested kb context with various query formats: keyword queries (swarm, dashboard) returned results, but semantic queries (swarm map sorting, how should dashboard present agents) returned nothing. The pattern reveals desire for semantic query answering that would require LLM-based RAG.

content: 24-hour threshold for Recent vs Archive in dashboard
reason: Balances operational focus (recent work visible) with history access (older work collapsed but accessible)

content: Investigations already do semantic query answering (kb context → read → synthesize). The 'recommend' pattern desire is for SYNCHRONOUS/INLINE answers without spawn overhead, not new capability.
reason: Dylan's reframe: every investigation spawn does exactly this. The friction is 30s+ spawn latency vs 5s inline answer. Consider 'kb ask' for mini-investigations without artifact overhead.

content: When should orchestrator use 'orch send' for follow-up vs spawn fresh investigation? What are session expiration limits, context degradation patterns, and phase reporting implications?
orch send vs spawn: use task relatedness not session age
reason: Sessions have no TTL, completed agents accept Q&A, context preserved at 51k chars. Technical constraints are non-issues; decision is semantic.

content: Agent card titles use TLDR for completed, task for active
reason: Human-readable titles convey gist at a glance without requiring users to parse workspace naming conventions

content: Usage display color thresholds: green <60%, yellow 60-80%, red >80%
reason: Matches established UX patterns for warning levels, consistent with how other dashboards signal utilization

content: Auto-switch uses 5-hour >80% and weekly >90% thresholds with 10% min headroom delta
reason: Conservative defaults prevent thrashing while ensuring switch when genuinely beneficial

content: Slide-out panel for agent card detail view
reason: Preserves swarm grid context while showing detail; standard pattern for select-to-detail UX; modal blocks view, inline expand disrupts grid

content: After agents commit Go changes, orchestrator should auto-rebuild and restart affected services
reason: Manual rebuild/restart is friction. Pattern: detect changed files (cmd/orch/, pkg/) → make install → restart orch serve if running. Could be hook or part of orch complete flow.

content: UI changes in web/ require visual verification via Playwright MCP screenshot
reason: Tests passing does not guarantee UI renders correctly. Visual evidence ensures feature works as intended.

content: Dashboard integrations tiered: Beads+Focus high, Servers medium, KB/KN skip
reason: Operational awareness purpose means actionable work queue > reference material

content: Orchestrator sessions should transition at 75-80% context usage
reason: At 78% (156k tokens), quality still good but approaching risk zone. Below 75% continue freely, above 85% responses may degrade. Use session-transition skill to capture state and create handoff.

content: Exclude Phase: Complete agents from concurrency limit
reason: Completed agents are idle and not consuming resources. Counting them against the limit blocks new spawns unnecessarily.

content: Parse workspace date from name suffix (DDmon format) for completed agent updated_at, with file modification time as fallback
reason: Enables proper sorting in archive section where completed agents lacked timestamps

content: orch clean default behavior is report-only
reason: Workspaces preserved for investigation reference; cleanup actions require explicit flags (--windows, --phantoms, --verify-opencode)

content: After agents commit Go changes, orchestrator should auto-rebuild and restart affected services
reason: Manual rebuild/restart is friction. Pattern: detect changed files (cmd/orch/, pkg/) -> make install -> restart orch serve if running. Could be hook or part of orch complete flow.

content: SSE events drive is_processing state updates for real-time agent card visualization
reason: Avoids polling by using session.status busy/idle events and message.part events to infer processing state client-side

content: onDestroy for window cleanup in SvelteKit
result: onDestroy runs during SSR, causes window is not defined error. Use onMount return function instead with browser guard.

content: Dashboard account name lookup uses email reverse-mapping from accounts.yaml
reason: Provides meaningful account identifier (personal/work) instead of ambiguous email prefix

content: Dashboard agent status derived from beads phase, not session time
reason: Phase: Complete from beads comments is authoritative for completion status, session idle time is secondary

content: GetAccountCapacity token comparison can fail when tokens drift between auth.json and accounts.yaml
reason: External token rotation (by OpenCode) causes isActiveAccount check to fail, leading to auth.json being left with stale tokens after rotation

content: Dashboard beads stats use bd stats --json API call
reason: Provides comprehensive issue statistics with ready/blocked/open counts in single call

content: Dashboard panel additions follow pattern: API endpoint in serve.go -> Svelte store -> page.svelte integration
reason: Established during focus/beads/servers panel additions Dec 24

content: Focus drift API uses CheckDrift from focus package
reason: Reuses existing pkg/focus CheckDrift() rather than duplicating drift detection logic

content: LLM guidance compliance requires signal balance - overwhelming counter-patterns (56:13 ratio) drowns specific exceptions
reason: Investigation found orchestrator skill has 4:1 ask-vs-act signal ratio causing autonomy guidance to fail

content: Use title attributes for tooltips in Svelte rather than custom tooltip component
reason: Native browser tooltips work well for simple explanatory text, no extra dependencies needed

content: Show full TLDR on completed agent cards by removing line-clamp-2
reason: Users want to see the complete summary at a glance instead of having to click for details

content: Show collapsed section preview
reason: Users can see agent tasks at a glance without expanding sections

content: Active agents should use stable sort (spawned_at) to prevent grid reordering from SSE updates
reason: updated_at changes every second for active agents, causing constant visual churn in the dashboard grid

content: Dashboard must be fully usable at 666px width (half MacBook Pro screen). No horizontal scrolling. All critical info visible without scrolling.
reason: Primary workflow is orchestrator CLI + dashboard side-by-side on MacBook Pro. Minimum width constraint - should expand gracefully on larger displays.

content: Usage progress bars show reset times inline with percentage
reason: Reset time context is essential for orchestrator to know when to switch accounts or wait

content: Check for displayable content (tldr, outcome, close_reason) before rendering synthesis section
reason: Prevents empty bordered sections from appearing when synthesis object exists but has no content

content: Use spawned_at for stable sort in both Active and Recent sections
reason: Prevents jostling when SSE updates modify updated_at; Archive can use volatile sort since historical recency matters there

content: Use beads close_reason as fallback when synthesis is null
reason: Light-tier agents don't create SYNTHESIS.md by design, but beads close_reason contains equivalent summary info

content: Monitor Vibium but stick with Playwright MCP
reason: Vibium (by Selenium creator) is promising but v1 too early (shipped Dec 22, 2024). Lacks JS eval, network monitoring, DOM inspection we need. Playwright MCP remains superior per .kb/investigations/simple/2025-12-02-side-side-comparison-browser-use.md. Revisit when Vibium v2 ships. GitHub: github.com/VibiumDev/vibium

content: orch-go auth implementation is complete
reason: Code review shows Go has OAuth login, refresh, switch, capacity tracking - all features from Python orch-cli

content: TokenRefreshError includes account name for actionable guidance
reason: Enables 'orch account remove X && orch account add X' suggestion when token refresh fails

content: Keep beads as external dependency with abstraction layer
reason: 7-command interface surface is narrow; dependency-first design (ready queue, dep graph) has no equivalent in alternatives; Phase 3 abstraction addresses API stability risk at low cost

content: BEADS_NO_DAEMON=1 in .zshrc only
result: Doesn't propagate to launchd agents or processes spawned before shell sourcing. Added to launchd plist as well.

content: bd exec.Command calls in orch-go are concurrency-safe
reason: Beads daemon serializes database access via Unix socket, GetCommentsBatch has semaphore limiting to 10 concurrent

content: pkg/beads uses direct RPC with CLI fallback
reason: Performance gains from skipping process spawn, graceful degradation when daemon unavailable

content: RPC-first fallback pattern for beads operations
reason: Sequential RPC calls through single connection are simpler than concurrent CLI subprocess spawning; daemon handles serialization so semaphore pattern is unnecessary

content: beads RPC migration pattern
reason: Try RPC client first, fall through to CLI on any error (socket not found, connect failed, operation failed) - graceful degradation

content: beads RPC client fallback pattern
reason: Try RPC client first, fallback to CLI on error - provides performance when daemon running, compatibility when not

content: Dashboard progressive disclosure is already fully implemented
reason: Active/Recent/Archive sections with 24h threshold, localStorage persistence, count badges, and preview text all exist in current codebase

content: Dashboard project filter follows skill filter pattern - state var, unique extraction, apply function, dropdown UI
reason: Consistent pattern makes future filter additions predictable and maintainable

content: Skill output verification parses skill.yaml directly
reason: skillc verify CLI doesn't exist, so we parse outputs.required from skill.yaml files in Go

content: KB context uses priority-based truncation: constraints > decisions > investigations
reason: Constraints are critical (must respect), decisions next (prior choices), investigations lowest (exploratory)

content: orch serve status uses health check not PID file
reason: Simpler, no state management needed, health check is authoritative for whether server is responding

content: Orchestrator is AI, not Dylan - Dylan interacts with AI orchestrators who spawn/complete agents
reason: Investigation 2025-12-25-design-orchestrator-completion-lifecycle-two incorrectly framed Dylan as the actor who spawns agents. The actor model is: Dylan ↔ AI Orchestrator ↔ Worker Agents. Mental model sync flows: Agent→Orchestrator (synthesis) and Orchestrator→Dylan (conversation).

content: Daemon completion polling preferred over SSE detection
reason: SSE busy->idle triggers false positives during normal agent operation; Phase: Complete is only reliable signal

content: SSE busy->idle cannot detect true agent completion
reason: Agents go idle during loading, thinking, waiting for tools - not just when done

content: Skill manifests should declare spawn requirements
reason: Makes skills self-contained and portable. Spawn reads manifest and honors declarations instead of hardcoding behavior.

content: Daemon completion uses beads polling not SSE
reason: SSE busy->idle detection has false positives during tool loading; Phase: Complete in beads comments is only reliable signal

content: Investigation filenames are generated from task description at spawn time
reason: generateSlug() runs at spawn before agent starts - cannot reflect findings in initial filename

content: Skills own domain behavior, spawn owns orchestration infrastructure
reason: Architect review found current separation is correct design, not fragmentation. Skills containing beads/phase logic would reduce portability and violate Compose Over Monolith. See .kb/investigations/2025-12-25-design-should-we-evolve-skills-where.md

content: Ask 'should we' before 'how do we' for strategic direction changes
reason: Epic orch-go-erdw was created assuming skills-as-value was correct direction. Architect review revealed the premise was wrong - current separation is intentional design. Wasted work avoided by validating premise before execution.

content: SHOULD-HOW-EXECUTE sequence for strategic questions
reason: Epic orch-go-erdw was created from 'how do we' without testing premise. Architect found premise wrong. Wasted work avoided if 'should we' asked first.

content: Complete agents before spawning more - maintain spawn:complete ratio
reason: Dec 25 reflection: 201 issues created vs 12 closed in 24h. Backlog grew faster than completion. 29 stuck agents accumulated. Spawn discipline prevents debt accumulation.

content: Pre-spawn context gathering (<5 min, for spawn prompts) is distinct from deep investigation (>15 min, delegate)
reason: Orchestrator skill conflated these; explicit boundary resolves contradiction. See .kb/investigations/2025-12-25-inv-orchestrator-pre-spawn-context-gathering.md

content: Dashboard uses SYNTHESIS.md as fallback for untracked agent completion detection
reason: Untracked agents have fake beads IDs that won't match real issues, so Phase: Complete check fails - workspace-based detection is the reliable fallback

content: orch status uses GetIssuesBatch to check closed status
reason: Provides both task info and closed status in single batch call, replacing ListOpenIssues which only returned open issues

content: orch complete surfaces synthesis recommendations before closing
reason: Ensures investigation follow-up items aren't lost during agent completion

content: 'orch review done' processes completions
reason: Previously only printed status, now actually closes beads issues and cleans up tmux. Addresses completion loop gap.

content: orch review done command complete
reason: Already implemented in commit 635a7c1 (Dec 25 2025). The spawn context was based on stale investigation data. Feature verified working: closes beads issues, cleans up tmux, logs events.

content: orch complete prompts for each SYNTHESIS.md recommendation individually
reason: Better UX than batch y/n - allows selective follow-up issue creation

content: Visual verification gate is already integrated into VerifyCompletionFull
reason: Found that VerifyVisualVerificationForCompletion is called in check.go:394-403, blocking completion when web/ files modified without evidence

content: Daemon-first for batch work
reason: Label issues triage:ready + skill:X, daemon auto-spawns. Orchestrator focuses on triage, labeling, synthesis, completion review. Manual orch spawn for single urgent items only.

content: No sacred cows - everything is replaceable at the right cost
reason: Load-bearing (Provenance, Session Amnesia) requires equivalent replacement. Implementation (beads, orch, kn, kb) requires something better. Convention (paths, naming) requires friction < value. Nothing is immune from questioning.

content: Don't spawn multiple agents to edit the same file
reason: Causes merge conflicts and wasted work. Consolidate into one prompt or spawn sequentially.

content: OpenCode serve requires --port 4096 flag
reason: Default is random port. Daemon, orch CLI, and dashboard all expect 4096.

content: kn entries can accumulate into skill changes
reason: Pattern: multiple related constraints/decisions on same topic → skill update. Example: daemon kn entries → skill launchd docs + daemon-first workflow. Not yet automated - manual recognition required.

content: Pressure Visibility System has 3 layers: Gap Detection, Failure Surfacing, System Learning Loop
reason: Operationalizes Pressure Over Compensation principle - each layer builds on previous

content: Gap detection uses quality scoring 0-100 with constraints weighted highest (25pts), then decisions (15pts), then investigations (10pts)
reason: Constraints are most critical for preventing incorrect agent behavior; investigations are least critical as they're exploratory

content: System Learning Loop uses RecurrenceThreshold=3
reason: 1 occurrence = noise, 2 = coincidence, 3+ = pattern worth addressing - industry standard heuristic

content: Proposal tables use ID/Target/Reason/Approved format for kb-reflect actions
reason: Enables quick orchestrator approval - scan table, mark [x] for approved rows

content: Cross-project agent visibility requires extracting PROJECT_DIR from SPAWN_CONTEXT.md
reason: Beads comments are per-project; to show phase info for agents from other projects, must query the correct project's beads database

content: Cross-project beads queries require PROJECT_DIR from workspace SPAWN_CONTEXT.md
reason: Beads issues are per-project; bd commands run in current directory; for cross-project agent visibility, must extract PROJECT_DIR and query correct project's .beads/ directory

content: cross project agent visibility requires fetching beads comments from agent's project directory, not orchestrator's current directory
reason: Agents spawned with --workdir run in different repos than orchestrator. bd comments uses cwd by default, missing the agent's actual beads issue.

content: Generate gap reasons from context
reason: Gap events contain skill, task, and occurrence data that can be formatted into meaningful kn entry reasons

content: spawn_requires uses *bool for optional boolean fields
reason: Distinguishes unset (nil, use default) from explicitly false; helper Get* methods handle nil gracefully

content: Runtime dependency resolution is preferred over compile-time for cross-directory skill dependencies
reason: skillc's graph validates dependencies exist within the same compilation context; cross-directory deps require orch-go's LoadSkillWithDependencies at spawn time

content: cross project agent visibility requires fetching beads comments from agent's project directory, not orchestrator's current directory
reason: Agents spawned with --workdir run in different repos than orchestrator. bd comments uses cwd by default, missing the agent's actual beads issue.

content: Activity type determines highlighting intensity
reason: Transient states (step-finish) should be visually minimal, while active work (tool, reasoning) deserves subtle attention

content: orch learn clear should be rare - prefer orch learn resolve for individual gaps
reason: Learning loop needs history to detect patterns. Clearing throws away recurring patterns, effectiveness tracking, and trend data. Use resolve for individual items; let 30-day auto-pruning handle the rest.

content: Usage stats consolidation moves reset times to header, removes duplicate stats bar row
reason: Reduces visual clutter while maintaining all information in single location

content: Use shadcn-svelte tooltip component with Tooltip.Provider in layout for SSR
reason: Built on existing bits-ui dependency, no new libs needed, consistent with project patterns

content: Agents modifying web/ files MUST capture visual verification via Playwright MCP before completing
reason: Tests passing does not verify UI renders correctly. Prior instances of UI features shipping with broken rendering despite passing tests.

content: 24 hours is the staleness threshold for orch review
reason: Agents stuck in non-Complete phase for >24h are effectively abandoned. Directory modification time is a reasonable proxy for activity.

content: System resource visibility not needed in orchestrator
reason: External monitoring (sketchybar) already provides CPU/memory visibility; high resource usage in orch processes indicates bugs to fix, not normal states to monitor

content: D.E.K.N. validation gates on synthesis sections only (Knowledge, Next)
reason: Delta and Evidence can be auto-populated from data; only synthesis requires human reflection

content: orch kb ask uses polling instead of SSE streaming for LLM response
reason: SSE streaming via SendMessageWithStreaming returned empty responses. Polling approach checks for completed assistant message in session messages every 500ms with 60s timeout. More reliable for short-lived synthesis queries.

content: RecordResolution must mark ALL matching events, not just most recent
reason: When resolving a gap pattern, all occurrences must be marked or they continue appearing in suggestions

content: Dashboard is_processing visual indicators require status === 'active' check
reason: SSE session.status events may not clear is_processing flag when agent completes, causing stale pulsing animation. Defensive check ensures only active agents show processing state.

content: Systems form around foundational constraints through accumulated compensations - same mechanism as evolution
reason: Observed in orch system: amnesia constraint generated externalization infrastructure, agreement constraint generated governance layer. Not designed top-down, shaped by pressure over time.

content: Mock daemon handlers must run in loop for persistent connections
reason: Client reuses single connection for health check + operations; returning after first request breaks subsequent calls

content: Shell-aware command parsing required for orch learn act
reason: strings.Fields breaks quoted arguments; must use custom parser respecting double/single quotes

content: Mock SSE EventSource with Playwright page.route for load testing
result: EventSource onopen never fires with mocked route response - SSE needs persistent connection

content: Use direct fetch in page.evaluate to test API performance
reason: Bypasses SSE connection requirement while still testing the mock API response time

content: Integration tests should use t.Skip when daemon not available
reason: Allows tests to pass in CI or environments without beads daemon

content: SSE event handlers must check agent status before setting is_processing=true
reason: Late SSE events can arrive after agent status changes, causing stale visual state

content: Use snap for simple visual verification, Playwright MCP for complex UI testing
reason: Playwright MCP tool definitions cost ~5-10k tokens. For basic 'take screenshot' verification in feature-impl, snap CLI is sufficient and nearly zero token cost. Reserve Playwright for: automated assertions, headless CI, complex interactions.

content: Multi-project workspace aggregation uses session.Directory for dynamic discovery
reason: Better than static registry - self-healing, no maintenance, scales naturally with active sessions

content: Could we derive CLAUDE.md empirically from orch learn gaps?
Monitor should delete sessions after completion
reason: Prevents memory leak in long-running daemons - sessions accumulate unboundedly otherwise

content: SSE reconnect should use loop pattern not goroutine spawning
reason: Spawning new goroutines on reconnect leaks goroutines - each reconnection accumulated 2 orphaned goroutines

content: CLI output must be readable at 80 chars (half MacBook screen)
reason: orch status now adapts to terminal width: wide (>120), narrow (80-100), and card (<80) formats

content: bd comment with untracked BeadsID
result: Synthetic IDs like orch-go-untracked-* don't exist in beads database - results in 'issue not found' error

content: Testing spawn context by having agent run bd comment with synthetic beads ID
result: Synthetic IDs (format: project-untracked-timestamp) don't exist in beads, so bd comment always fails for --no-track spawns

content: OpenCode handles OAuth auto-refresh via anthropic-auth plugin
reason: Code inspection shows token check at fetch time - no orch implementation needed

content: Ethical questions sometimes dissolve when you find the real question underneath
reason: The ToS violation question dissolved into 'will I ask for what I need?' The surface question was about rules; the real question was about avoiding vulnerability of asking.

content: Surface Before Circumvent: Before working around a constraint, surface it to the people involved
reason: The accountability is a feature, not a cost. Extends Pressure Over Compensation from systems to relationships. Discovered when ToS violation path would have bypassed asking Jim for token budget.

content: OpenCode serve runs via launchd with KeepAlive for auto-restart
reason: Foundation service - orch daemon and serve depend on it. Located at ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.opencode.serve.plist

content: generateReasonFromGaps must ensure 20+ char minimum for kn compatibility
reason: The kn command requires --reason to be at least 20 characters. When gap events lack skill/task metadata, the base reason was too short.

content: Use native Go RPC client for beads integration instead of CLI subprocess
reason: Eliminates subprocess overhead, provides type safety, enables proper error handling. Socket at .beads/bd.sock. See .kb/investigations/2025-12-25-inv-design-beads-integration-strategy-orch.md

content: Dashboard gets lightweight acknowledgment actions (approve, reject, mark reviewed, priority bump); orchestrator keeps reasoning actions (spawn, synthesize, scope)
reason: Control separation: if it requires judgment, orchestrator. If it's confirmation of something already decided, dashboard. Reduces context switching without blurring the thinking/seeing boundary.

content: Land the plane: Transition sessions at ~50-60% context while thinking is still clear, not at 80%+ when scrambling
reason: Handoff quality degrades as context fills. Clean stopping points produce better session resumption than forced stops. Stop while you can still think clearly.

content: Dashboard daemon indicator uses file-based status (daemon-status.json) over IPC
reason: Simple, decoupled approach - daemon writes status to file on each poll, dashboard reads it. No process coupling, works when daemon restarts, allows monitoring from any process.

content: Context waste from debug-retry loops - is fix in orch ecosystem or opencode tool instructions?
Dashboard synthesis review shows synthesis inline with actionable issue creation
reason: Enables orchestrators to act on synthesis recommendations without leaving dashboard UI

content: checking if /api/reflect endpoint needed implementation
result: endpoint was already added in commit 88b71037 along with /api/gaps endpoint - commit message only mentioned gaps but included reflect

content: Issue orch-go-zxy5 had implementation spec but unclear design - agent implemented buttons without review workflow, didn't solve actual problem
result: Feature-impl completed literal spec but missed the point - need design-session first for UX/workflow problems

content: Reactive approach applies to reversible failures; irreversible failures (data loss, state corruption) warrant proactive auditing
reason: Pressure Over Compensation assumes failures are learning signals, but irreversible failures can't teach - the damage is done. Distinguish failure severity when applying the principle.

content: Detection accelerates pressure, prevention relieves it - audits and health checks are detection (surfaces failures faster), not compensation (prevents failures from occurring)
reason: Pressure Over Compensation should reject prevention, not detection. orch doctor is detection - it makes failures visible immediately rather than waiting for downstream breakage. The failure still happens, the learning signal is preserved, but latency is reduced.

content: Reactive infrastructure approach with orch doctor detection
reason: Aligns with pressure-over-compensation principle - failures are signals that tell us which services need KeepAlive, not problems to prevent proactively

content: What artifact type should capture evolving landscape maps (entities, positioning, relationships over time)? Investigations are point-in-time, guides are procedures, decisions are choices. Maps/landscapes are a distinct knowledge type - tracking a domain, not answering a question.
orch review done prompts for synthesis recommendations before closing
reason: Surfaces agent follow-up recommendations to orchestrator during batch completion, preventing value leak

content: Error pattern analysis uses normalized message matching for grouping similar errors
reason: Enables dashboard to show recurring patterns by truncating to 100 chars and trimming whitespace

content: Dashboard queue visibility should use expandable section under stats bar
reason: Consistent with CollapsibleSection pattern, respects 666px constraint, no context switching required

content: orch doctor checks OpenCode (4096), orch serve (3348), and beads daemon
reason: Centralized service health check makes it easy to diagnose infrastructure issues

content: Auto-switch already implemented in account.AutoSwitchIfNeeded and integrated at spawn time in runSpawnWithSkill
reason: Found existing implementation during investigation - checkAndAutoSwitchAccount at main.go:975 calls AutoSwitchIfNeeded which handles the 80% threshold check and account switching

content: Global user config in pkg/userconfig reads ~/.orch/config.yaml
reason: Needed unified place for user preferences like notifications. Follows same pattern as pkg/account for ~/.orch/accounts.yaml.

content: How should system detect when new commands/features need skill documentation updates? Current gap: orch-go-iw2i added 'orch doctor' but nothing prompted skill update - orchestrator manually compensated.
Store review state in per-workspace .review-state.json
reason: Enables cleanup when workspace is deleted, keeps state close to synthesis

content: Ready queue UI uses truncate for titles and limit 2 labels shown
reason: Keeps queue items scannable at 666px width

content: Use WithAutoReconnect(3) for beads RPC clients in daemon
reason: Provides resilience against daemon restarts and transient connection issues, matching the pattern already established in pkg/beads

content: Use persistent beads RPC client with WithAutoReconnect for serve.go handlers
reason: Reduces per-request connection overhead and handles daemon restarts gracefully

content: Use WithAutoReconnect(3) for beads RPC client in verify package
reason: Simplifies code by removing manual Connect/Close calls and adds resilience through auto-reconnection on connection errors

content: make install-restart chains install with daemon kickstart
reason: Convenience target for developers who want daemon to use new binary immediately

content: snap CLI for debugging capture, Playwright MCP for web validation
reason: Web validation requires browser control (navigate, wait, interact) before capture - snap only provides capture

content: html-to-markdown v2 requires base + commonmark plugins
reason: The NewConverter() without plugins produces no output - must register plugins explicitly

content: Deduplicate agents by title in serve.go keeping most recent session
reason: OpenCode can have multiple sessions with same title (resumed agents). Dashboard shows duplicate entries without deduplication.

content: 5 second debounce for is_processing state
reason: Prevents gold border flashing between rapid tool calls. Agent switches busy/idle every ~100-500ms during active work.

content: Feature-impl light tier is intentional, review tooling needs update
reason: Tier system deliberately separates knowledge-producing skills (full) from implementation-focused skills (light). Review tooling only scans SYNTHESIS.md, creating blind spot for light tier completions.

content: ListSessions returns historical sessions - always use IsSessionActive for liveness checks
reason: OpenCode persists sessions to disk, so session existence doesn't mean it's running

content: Auto-detect new CLI commands by checking git status for Added (A) files in cmd/orch/*.go that contain cobra.Command patterns
reason: Follows existing hasGoChangesInRecentCommits pattern, enables timely documentation prompts

content: Light tier completions detected via .tier file + Phase: Complete in beads comments
reason: SYNTHESIS.md is not produced for light tier spawns, so we need alternative completion detection

content: ReadyQueueSection follows PendingReviewsSection pattern for dedicated dashboard sections
reason: Consistent component architecture, each section handles its own store subscription and toggle state

content: Auto-detect CLI commands feature was already complete
reason: Found detectNewCLICommands at main.go:3082-3150 with tests passing - no additional work needed

content: Use Playwright MCP for web/ visual verification, snap CLI for native macOS capture only
reason: Web validation requires browser control (navigate, wait, interact) before capture - snap only provides capture step

content: bits-ui 1.8.0 uses GroupHeading instead of Label, RadioItem children snippet receives {checked} parameter
reason: Discovered during dropdown-menu component creation - different from older shadcn-svelte examples

content: orch abandon uses same --workdir pattern as spawn for cross-project operations
reason: Consistency with spawn command; beads.DefaultDir enables socket discovery in target project

content: Cross-project beads ID detection uses project prefix comparison
reason: Consistent with abandon command pattern; extracts project from beads ID (e.g., kb-cli from kb-cli-xyz) and compares with current directory name

content: skillc binary is in ~/go/bin
reason: Not in default PATH, needs explicit path or PATH update

content: Verify issue status before starting work
reason: Previous agent completed work (commit 70b2e247) but issue remained in_progress. Check bd show and git log before implementing to avoid duplicate effort.

content: serve.go beads calls already use RPC via verify package
reason: ListOpenIssues, GetIssuesBatch, GetCommentsBatchWithProjectDirs all use beads.NewClient with auto-reconnect internally

content: OpenCode has no /health endpoint - use /session to check server status
reason: OpenCode returns 500 'redirected too many times' for any invalid route including /health. The valid endpoints are /session, /session/{id}, /session/{id}/message, etc.

content: Use generics for retry helper
reason: Retry[T any] pattern enables type-safe retry logic without interface{} casts. Applied to spawn error handling.

content: Surface Before Circumvent mechanism in spawn context uses bd comment CONSTRAINT: prefix for tracking
reason: Provides searchable, structured format for constraint surfacing. Pattern is bd comment <id> 'CONSTRAINT: [what] - [why]'

content: html-to-markdown v2 requires explicit plugin registration (base + commonmark) and WithDomain is a ConvertOptionFunc for ConvertString, not a converter option
reason: v2 API breaking change from v1

content: IsSessionActive should be used for liveness detection, not SessionExists
reason: OpenCode persists sessions to disk, so SessionExists returns true for historical sessions

content: HTTP clients for external APIs should always have timeouts
reason: http.DefaultClient has no timeout which can cause indefinite hangs when servers are unresponsive or in redirect loops

content: Skills with required: true file deliverables need outputs.required; skills with optional deliverables don't
reason: orch complete uses outputs.required to verify file artifacts exist

content: kb extract uses inline lineage metadata (extracted-from, source-project, extraction-date)
reason: Session Amnesia principle requires self-describing artifacts; fresh agents can trace artifact lineage from inline comments

content: kb supersede command uses regex pattern matching to update lineage headers in markdown files
reason: Allows flexible matching of existing header values (empty or populated) and handles files with or without lineage blocks

content: Agent registry removal complete - all state from OpenCode API + beads
reason: Phase 4 complete: removed registry package, documentation cleaned up. No agent-registry.json file needed.

content: skillc is at ~/Documents/personal/skillc/bin/skillc, not in PATH
reason: Found during skill rebuild - binary exists but not symlinked

content: Track fix attempts via events.jsonl scanning
reason: Events.jsonl already logs session.spawned, agent.abandoned, agent.completed with beads_id - no new storage needed for retry pattern detection

content: Visual intent clarification should be spawn-time discipline
reason: Enforcement at spawn prevents ambiguity from entering system; worker phases are optional and late; dedicated agents add overhead without addressing root cause

content: kb reflect --type refine detects kn entries that refine principles using keyword matching (2+ terms required)
reason: Keyword matching is simple, maintainable, and works well with existing kn/kb data structures

content: Theme JSONs use reference resolution pattern (defs + variant objects)
reason: Ported from OpenCode - resolution logic needed for refs to defs, theme properties, and dark/light variants

content: All CLI Makefiles should install to ~/bin, not ~/go/bin
reason: Prevents PATH shadowing - ~/go/bin may have stale binaries with higher PATH priority

content: Playwright MCP extension mode for shared browser
reason: Simplest integration path - human installs extension, agent connects to their actual browser session, no sync infrastructure needed

content: Playwright MCP architecture is near-optimal for agent browser automation
reason: LLM decision loop is the bottleneck (70-90% of time), not MCP protocol (<1%) or browser overhead (5-20%). Accessibility tree approach is 10-100x more efficient than screenshots.

content: 10x browser automation speedup requires agent architecture changes, not protocol optimization
reason: Action batching (3-5x), parallel speculation (2-3x), and action macros (2x for repeated flows) can combine for ~12x improvement. Protocol/browser layers are already optimized.

content: orch complete auto-detects cross-project agents from workspace SPAWN_CONTEXT.md PROJECT_DIR
reason: Follows same pattern as orch abandon --workdir; workspace metadata is authoritative source

content: requestIdleCallback is the right pattern for deferring non-critical UI data fetches in SvelteKit
reason: Browser-native scheduling ensures secondary data doesn't block initial render while still loading promptly

content: Light-tier pending reviews are grouped by default in Swarm Dashboard
reason: 153 identical cards from light-tier spawns were overwhelming - grouping with 'Dismiss All' reduces cognitive load while preserving ability to act on individual items if needed

content: Separate user observations from hypotheses before spawning
reason: Evidence Hierarchy: observations are primary evidence, hypotheses are secondary claims to verify. User saying 'hydration is slow' contains observation (slow) and hypothesis (hydration) that must be parsed.

content: Dylan doesn't interact with dashboard directly - orchestrator uses Glass for all browser interactions
reason: Pressure Over Compensation: forces gaps in Glass tooling to surface rather than routing around them

content: UI features require browser verification before marking complete
reason: Post-mortem og-work-dashboard-two-modes-27dec: SSR hydration bug passed build/typecheck but failed in browser; agent documented 'not browser-tested' but had no gate to force validation

content: SvelteKit stores using browser APIs must use init() pattern with onMount
reason: SSR initializes module at server-side where window is undefined; store instance persists through hydration, so initialization code must be called explicitly in onMount

content: MCP for agent-internal use, CLI for orchestrator/scripts/humans
reason: MCP tools only available during agent sessions. Orchestrator, validation gates, and scripts need CLI interface. Glass needs both: MCP for agents doing UI work, CLI for orch complete validation.

content: Glass assert command for orchestrator validation
reason: CLI provides exit codes (0/1) for scripting in orch complete workflow - MCP requires agent context

content: Tool action outcomes are ephemeral - cannot detect behavioral patterns without action logging
reason: Investigation found learning.go tracks knowledge gaps, not action outcomes. Self-correction requires observing action→outcome sequences, which are currently transient.

content: Pattern analyzer uses 7-day retention vs 30-day for gap tracking
reason: Behavioral patterns are more ephemeral than knowledge gaps - actions happen frequently, patterns become stale faster

content: Skill selection judgment preserved at issue creation not labeling
reason: Daemon uses InferSkill(issueType) only; skill:* labels not implemented. Type→skill mapping is reliable. Batch-labeling doesn't sacrifice judgment.

content: Action logging subsystem uses action-log.jsonl
reason: Consistent with events.jsonl and gap-tracker.json patterns in ~/.orch/

content: Skill changes route by blast radius + change type
reason: Decision tree based on 60+ skill commits: infrastructure=design-session, local=direct, cross-skill depends on implicit dependencies

content: 3 concurrent daemon spawns
result: CPU hit 99%, had to abandon all agents

content: orch patterns command surfaces behavioral patterns for orchestrator awareness
reason: Command helps avoid blind respawning by detecting retry, persistent_failure, empty_context, recurring_gap, and futile_action patterns

content: skillc is in ~/go/bin, not in PATH by default
reason: Needed to compile orchestrator skill changes

content: skillc is in ~/go/bin, not ~/bin
reason: Found when trying to build orchestrator skill - skillc binary built with Go so lives in go/bin

content: skillc deploy requires --target flag
reason: Different from older skillc build workflow

content: 5-tier completion escalation model: None, Info, Review, Block, Failed
reason: Enables auto-completion for routine work (~60%) while surfacing knowledge-producing skills and recommendations for human review (~30%), with blocking for failures and approvals (~10%)

content: 5-tier escalation model integrated into daemon ProcessCompletion
reason: Enables auto-completion for ~60% of routine work (None/Info/Review levels) while blocking completions requiring human decision (Block level for visual approval, Failed level for verification errors)

content: Up Next section auto-expands on P0/P1
reason: Urgent items should be surfaced proactively without user interaction

content: Light tier detection in orch review uses isLightTierComplete() from serve.go
reason: Reuses existing light tier detection logic for consistency between API and CLI

content: Playwright MCP viewport should use --viewport-size=1440x900 to match user display
reason: Prevents visual drift where agent screenshots show different layouts than user views. The 1440x900 matches Dylan's browseruse config and typical MacBook display.

content: Dashboard agent card derives displayState from status + phase + activity timestamp for clearer visual distinction
reason: Addresses ambiguity where active + 'Waiting for activity' was unclear - now shows 'ready-for-review' (blue), 'idle' (orange), or 'running' (yellow) based on combined state

content: Agent cards should use lift effect on hover
reason: Provides tactile feedback for clickable elements without being distracting

content: Glass dogfooding requires Chrome launched with --remote-debugging-port=9222 WITHOUT --user-data-dir flag
reason: Using --user-data-dir creates isolated profile, breaking 'see what agent sees' UX. Default launch should use primary profile.

content: Dashboard requires TWO servers: 1) orch serve (API on 3348), 2) bun run dev in web/ (UI on 5173+). The API alone returns 404 for UI routes.
reason: Keep forgetting dashboard is split frontend/backend

content: Cross-project gap capture uses SourceProject field on GapEvent to track which project discovered the gap
reason: Enables filtering gaps by source project for dogfooding improvements back to orch-go. Gap tracker was already global so only metadata was needed.

content: kn init integrated into orch init by default
reason: Ensures .kn/ directory is created alongside .kb/ and .beads/ for complete orchestration scaffolding

content: Architect recommendations surfaced in orch status with rich detail
reason: SessionStart surfacing creates pressure to review high-value design work

content: kb learn owns gap tracking
reason: Phase 1 consolidation - learning loop moved from orch to kb because it's about knowledge gaps and suggests kn/kb artifacts

content: Start orchestrator sessions from orch-go directory, not glass or other projects with MCP servers. MCP servers are session-scoped and will pollute orchestrator context.
reason: Started in glass, Playwright MCP followed to orch-go orchestration, causing confusion

content: Dev servers: launchd for native processes, Docker restart policies for containers, manual switchboard (orch servers up/down)
reason: Operational invisibility via OS-native launchd, explicit control via switchboard, no always-running overhead

content: kb learn owns the learning loop
reason: Phase 1 consolidation complete - orch learn now delegates to kb learn, gap-tracker.json moved to ~/.kb/

content: SessionStart surfacing is implemented via --session-start flag to existing commands, not separate hooks
reason: Integrating with existing CLI commands is simpler than creating new hook infrastructure - the flag can be called by any hook system (Claude Code, launchd, or manual scripts)

content: Epic child issues should have triage:review label not triage:ready
reason: Orchestrator should review before daemon picks up - allows adjusting skill selection or priority

content: Provenance system should be tiered: session ID (minimal), sources (standard), full thread (rich)
reason: Matches existing tier pattern (light/full spawns), balances transparency with overhead, session ID enables full recovery when needed

content: orch spawn --mcp flag is metadata-only
reason: OpenCode MCP servers are configured via opencode.json not CLI flags. Flag tracks intent but does not actually enable MCP.

content: spawned for cross-repo work without first verifying work completion
result: orch-knowledge template retirement was already completed (commit 7430185), and the repo no longer exists

content: stderr capture in headless spawns
reason: Previously stderr was discarded, now captured and logged for error visibility

content: Glass tools should use glass_* naming pattern for MCP integration
reason: Matches Playwright convention (browser_*), enables pattern-based detection for visual verification

content: ActionEvent struct and action-log.jsonl persistence already implemented in pkg/action/
reason: Prior agent orch-go-4oh7.1 completed this work. Verified 14 tests passing and integration with orch patterns command.

content: kb-cli investigationTemplate verified synced with ~/.kb/templates/INVESTIGATION.md
reason: Verification on 2025-12-27 confirmed templates match. Differences are only Go string escaping for backticks, not content. Commit 032ea18 performed the sync.

content: Glass binary at ~/bin/glass was corrupted
result: Returned exit 137 (SIGKILL) with no output despite identical checksum to working copy; replaced binary fixed the issue

content: Use absolute path for MCP command binaries
reason: npx-based playwright MCP fails when npx not in PATH; glass uses absolute path and is more reliable

content: skillc should remain separate from orch-go
reason: Different purposes (skill compilation vs agent orchestration), works well independently, merging would complicate both tools

content: agentlog for AI-native error visibility
result: Never integrated into workflow - 30 skill references but tool not installed. Good concept, incomplete implementation.

content: Clean up tool references before archiving
reason: Dead references in skills cause confusion. Verify tool is actually installed/used before referencing in docs.

content: MCP tools should NOT include client name prefix in their names - opencode adds it automatically
reason: OpenCode's MCP integration prefixes all tool names with {client}_{toolname}, so glass_tabs becomes glass_glass_tabs

content: Page.CaptureScreenshot returns bytes directly, not base64 - mafredri/cdp handles encoding
reason: CDP result.Data is already []byte, need to base64 encode for MCP ImageContent

content: Dashboard search filter uses case-insensitive matching across id, beads_id, task, beads_title, skill, project fields
reason: Comprehensive coverage for finding agents by any identifying attribute

content: servers.yaml uses list of server objects with type-specific fields
reason: Richer schema than flat map allows health checks, dependencies, and different server types

content: launchd plists use /bin/sh -c wrapper for complex commands
reason: Shell commands with pipes or arguments need wrapping in shell to execute properly as launchd ProgramArguments

content: orch servers switchboard uses launchd for command-type, Docker for docker-type
reason: Native macOS service management for reliability and auto-restart

content: kb reflect --type skill-candidate clusters kn entries by topic keyword, with 3+ threshold
reason: Consistent with synthesis threshold, keyword-based clustering is more reliable than NLP for operational domains

content: When spawning research comparing external tools to local projects, explicitly describe what the local project is and where it lives
reason: Research agent tried to GitHub search for Glass instead of knowing it's a local CDP-based browser automation project

content: SSE Stream section should show expandable event rows with full JSON
reason: Addresses issue of SSE events section showing count but no way to view actual content

content: orch sessions uses hybrid disk+API approach
reason: Disk walk is fast for listing, API provides full message content for search

content: Action logging integration uses OpenCode tool.execute.after plugin hook
reason: OpenCode's plugin system provides designed extension point for tool observation; SSE events lack tool output, post-session parsing is too late

content: orch next should synthesize signals rather than just show basic focus suggestions
reason: The orchestrator needs actionable recommendations that combine blockers, focus-aligned work, maintenance, and backlog - not just 'set-focus' or 'continue' prompts
